**Range** 60 ft.

**Reload** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

---

Sometimes referred to as an alley bow by rogues or ruffians, this small crossbow fires small bolts that are sometimes used to deliver poison to the target. It’s small enough to be shot one-handed, but it still requires two hands to load.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-3" />

[Composite Longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=74)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Deadly d10" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=174" />
<trait label="Propulsive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=191" />
<trait label="Volley 30 ft." url="/Traits.aspx?ID=201" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 282

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 20 gp

**Damage** 1d8 P

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1+

**Range** 100 ft.

**Reload** 0
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Ranged

**Category** Martial

**Group** [Bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3)
</row>

**Ammunition** [Arrows](/Weapons.aspx?ID=83)

</column>

---

This projectile weapon is made from horn, wood, and sinew laminated together to increase the power of its pull and the force of its projectile. Like all longbows, its great size also increases the bow’s range and power. You must use two hands to fire it, and it cannot be used while mounted. Any time an ability is specifically restricted to a longbow, such as Erastil’s favored weapon, it also applies to composite longbows unless otherwise stated.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-3" />

[Mammoth Bow](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1531), [Oathbow](/Equipment.aspx?ID=392)

    level="1"
    right="Weapon 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Polytool](/Weapons.aspx?ID=100)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Agile" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=170" />
<trait label="Modular B, P, or S" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=271" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gods & Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=17) pg. 121, [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 27

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Numeria have access to the polytool_

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 10 gp

**Damage** 1d6 modular

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Melee

**Category** Martial

**Group** [Sword](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=15)
</row>

</column>

---

The polytool is a small metal rod with a number of simple tools folded inside. The user can extend a long ceramic blade, as well as an awl, a chisel, a file, flint and steel, a hook, an inkpen, a magnifying glass, pliers, scissors, and a small saw. The flint and steel can be used up to 10 times before needing to be replaced. Though inspired by advanced Numerian technology, the polytool is a simple enough feat of metalworking that any blacksmith could produce it— perfect for the goddess [Casandalee](/Deities.aspx?ID=34) to spread innovation farther than actual Numerian tech could reach.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-15" />
name: Polytool

    level="1"
    right="Weapon 0"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Bladed Hoop](/Weapons.aspx?ID=116)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Finesse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=179" />
<trait label="Sweep" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=194" />
<trait label="Two-Hand 1d8" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=198" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #151: The Show Must Go On](/Sources.aspx?ID=21) pg. 77

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 2 gp, 5 sp

**Damage** 1d6 S

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 0+

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Melee

**Category** Advanced

**Group** [Knife](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=9)
</row>

</column>

---

This circular hoop has blades along its outer edge. You can wield it in two hands (using the two-hand damage) or by spinning it around an arm. While you spin the hoop, it gains the [free-hand](/Traits.aspx?ID=181) trait. Setting the hoop spinning takes 1 Interact action. In addition to the normal restrictions of the free-hand trait, extended use of the arm for locomotion (such as to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33)) interferes with the hoop’s spinning and forces you to Release the hoop. You can’t spin a hoop underwater. Keeping the hoop spinning requires a free action each round, which has the concentrate and manipulate traits. You can keep a hoop spinning as an exploration activity, but doing so for more than 10 minutes makes you fatigued, similarly to [Hustling](/Activities.aspx?ID=5).

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-9" />
name: Bladed Hoop

    right="Weapon 0"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Fire Poi](/Weapons.aspx?ID=117)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Agile" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=170" />
<trait label="Backswing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=173" />
<trait label="Finesse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=179" />
<trait label="Twin" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=197" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #151: The Show Must Go On](/Sources.aspx?ID=21) pg. 77

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 5 gp

**Damage** 1d4 B + 1d4 F

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Melee

**Category** Advanced

**Group** [Flail](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=7)
</row>

</column>

---

These special poi are made from a rare, light metal or from fire-retardant fibers and can be ignited before being wielded. Igniting a pair of fire poi is an Interact action and requires 1 pint of [oil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=38) for every 10 minutes the poi remain ignited. While lit, fire poi cast dim light in a 10-foot radius; in combat, they deal 1d4 bludgeoning plus 1d4 fire damage. On a critical hit with a lit fire poi, the target takes 1 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). The fire can be extinguished using the Interact action. When unlit, the poi deal only the listed bludgeoning damage. Regardless of whether it is lit, the poi’s 1d4 bludgeoning damage is the weapon damage dice, so _striking_ runes and other effects don’t affect the fire damage.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-7" />

<title
    level="1"
    right="Weapon 0"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Alchemical Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=118)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from The Fall of Plaguestone</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [The Fall of Plaguestone](/Sources.aspx?ID=3) pg. 56

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 25 gp

**Damage** 1d8 P

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 2

**Range** 30 ft.

**Reload** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Ranged

**Category** Simple

**Group** [Bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3)
</row>

</column>

---

This crossbow can deliver alchemically infused bolts. The strange weapon has a metal bracket mounted on the side of the stock near the lath. As an action, you can load a single lesser alchemical bomb into the bracket; this bomb must be one that deals energy damage (such as an [acid flask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=74), [alchemist’s fire](/Equipment.aspx?ID=75), [bottled lightning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=76), [frost vial](/Equipment.aspx?ID=77), or [thunderstone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=79)). The next three attacks made with the crossbow deal 1d6 damage of the bomb’s damage type in addition to the crossbow’s normal damage. If the second and third attacks are not all made within 1 minute of the first attack, the bomb’s energy is wasted. These attacks never deal splash damage or other special effects of the bomb and are not modified by any abilities that add to or modify a bomb’s effect. The addition of the bracket serves to unbalance the weapon, reducing its range to 30 feet. It otherwise functions as a crossbow (when determining damage, reload, and so on). Creatures use their crossbow proficiency when using the alchemical crossbow. The alchemical crossbow costs 25 gp.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-3" />
name: Alchemical Crossbow

Also called a “slungshot,” the monkey's fist is a short length of rope ending in a thick knot wrapped around a metal weight. You can tie the loose end of a monkey's fist to your wrist; if you are [Disarmed](/Actions.aspx?ID=41), the weapon remains secured to your wrist rather than falling to the ground, though you must use an Interact action to grip it before using it again. A monkey's fist is a martial melee weapon.

This collapsible baton consists of several nested, telescoping metal shafts that can be extended as a free action by flicking the wrist. Once extended to its full length (usually around 2 feet), the baton locks into shape until the wielder uses an Interact action to collapse it—a collapsed nightstick is 8 inches long, making it easily concealed. Lighter and more maneuverable than an ordinary club or truncheon, nightsticks are designed to subdue foes without causing permanent injury. A nightstick is an uncommon simple melee weapon.

This weapon is a fully functional, lavish ink pen with a clip that can be attached to a pocket or bandoleer and easily retrieved as a free action. ---

A common cutting tool, an adze resembles an axe—but the cutting edge is horizontal, rather than vertical. The adze's shape makes it popular among wood workers, and [grippli](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=110) builders often use them to construct their treetop homes. The tool also serves as an effective weapon, due in part to the immense strength required to swing it.

This [kobold](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=18) wire is thin and hard to see, making it perfect for an ambush. The wielder wraps the wire around a vulnerable spot, such as the neck, and twists it to inflict potentially fatal lacerations. The name derives from the use of a similar wire in traps, or slow fangs, with the word “slow” removed, as a fangwire gets quick results.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conrasu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=343" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 10 gp

**Bulk** 1

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 2

**Range** 100 ft.

**Reload** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Ranged

**Category** Advanced

**Group** [Bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3)
</row>

**Ammunition** [Wooden Taws](/Weapons.aspx?ID=152)

</column>

---

Designed by conrasu warriors, this complex device is a crossbow and fires small wooden bullets known as taws. A system of blades within the launcher can rapidly reshape a taw as it's loaded, allowing the launcher to fire taws of different shapes, such as fléchettes.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

---

[Tengu](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=21) use these specialized slings to fire darts further and with greater force than when thrown by hand, which is particularly effective when attacking from great heights. A thunder sling uses darts as ammunition. It can also hurl blowgun darts as ammunition but deals 1d4 piercing damage instead of 1d6 when used this way.

This hard but flexible longsword-length piece of wood looks more like a pole than a weapon, but can be deadly in the right hands. While generally not used for combat outside of Song’o culture, some halflings have become so proficient as to make it just as effective as a blade in a fight. Many halflings will even sing to maintain a certain tempo and rhythm during combat. It deals 1d6 bludgeoning damage. A fighting stick is a martial one-handed melee weapon in the club weapon group

This umbrella's ferrule is a hollow-tipped blade three to four inches in length and often overlooked as decorative. A receptacle inside the umbrella's shaft can be loaded with a single dose of [injury](/Traits.aspx?ID=97) [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) and injected into a damaged target with the pull of a sliding trigger. Reinforced ribs enable you to parry and deflect blows with the umbrella's tear-resistant canopy.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Deadly d6" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=174" />

<trait label="Reach" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=192" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 63

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 2 gp

**Damage** 1d4 B

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Melee

**Category** Martial

**Group** [Brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4)
</row>

</column>

---

A pantograph gauntlet is a heavy, fist-like weight, mounted on an extendable frame and attached to your outer arm with a series of leather straps. The frame's set of mechanical linkages connected at various hinges allow movements to propagate across the frame based on reshaping parallelograms, further controlled by a crossbar grasped in your hand. A pantograph gauntlet is driven by your own motion and mirrors your arm's movements—a punch thrown with your fist moves the pantograph, extending the weight out at a rapid speed to land blows up to 10 feet away. In some regions, such as Alkenstar and Ustalav, pantograph gauntlets are occasionally constructed entirely of metal and fashioned in the likeness of oversized arms, incorporating a complex system of gears or a miniature steam engine in place of the simpler pantograph mechanism.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

    right="Weapon 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Backpack Ballista](/Weapons.aspx?ID=173)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 62

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 18 gp

**Damage** 1d12 P

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1+

**Range** 180 ft.

**Reload** 1 minute
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Ranged

**Category** Martial

**Group** [Bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3)
</row>

**Ammunition** [Backpack Ballista Bolts](/Weapons.aspx?ID=179)

</column>

---

This complex wooden device, worn on the back, contains a miniature ballista on a retractable arm. As an Interact action, you can pull a lever to deploy or retract the ballista. As long as it remains deployed, you must hold the ballista using that hand or some of the components spill out onto the ground, just like dropping any other weapon. While deployed, the device opens and raises the ballista up over your shoulder. While retracted, the ballista and its mount slide down and are concealed within the device. Although a backpack ballista packs a punch, the device is a challenge to operate. Reloading it takes 1 minute and can't be done while worn. As normal, you can't wear a backpack ballista with another backpack.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-3" />

>
[Backpack Catapult](/Weapons.aspx?ID=174)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Volley 50 ft." url="/Traits.aspx?ID=201" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 63

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 20 gp

**Damage** 1d12 B

**Bulk** 3
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1+

**Range** 240 ft.

**Reload** 1 minute
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Ranged

**Category** Advanced

**Group** [Sling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=13)
</row>

**Ammunition** [Backpack Catapult Stones](/Weapons.aspx?ID=180)

</column>

---

This wooden device is worn on the back and contains a miniature catapult mounted on a retractable frame. As an Interact action, you can pull a lever to deploy or retract the catapult. As long as it remains deployed, you must hold the catapult using that hand or some of the components spill out onto the ground, just like dropping any other weapon. While deployed, the device opens and raises the catapult up over your shoulder. While retracted, the catapult and its mount slide down and are concealed within the device. A backpack catapult fires specialized stone spheres that are loaded into the bucket while unworn and retracted, through a sliding hatch; the reloading process takes 1 minute. To prevent misfires and accidental injury, the bucket fully encloses the stone while deployed, only opening when fired. As normal, you can't wear a backpack catapult with another backpack.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-13" />

<title
    level="1"
    right="Weapon 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Forked Bipod](/Weapons.aspx?ID=184)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Agile" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=170" />
<trait label="Deadly d6" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=174" />
<trait label="Finesse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=179" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 152

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon weapons, ammunition, and related items from Chapter 4_

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 3 sp

**Damage** 1d4 P

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Melee

**Category** Simple

**Group** [Spear](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=14)
</row>

</column>

---

Developed by Alkenstar scouts who often don't have time to break down a tripod when beset by the chaotic mutant monsters of the Mana Wastes, this two-pronged stabbing weapon can be used as a bipod to stabilize a gun with potent kickback. A forked bipod can be deployed or retrieved for use as a melee weapon as an Interact action. As with a [tripod](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1207), you can change your grip on the weapon as part of this action.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-14" />
name: Forked Bipod

>
[Bayonet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=186)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Agile" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=170" />
<trait label="Attached to crossbow or firearm" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=171" />
<trait label="Finesse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=179" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 152

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon weapons, ammunition, and related items from Chapter 4_

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 2 sp

**Damage** 1d4 P

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1 or 2

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Melee

**Category** Martial

**Group** [Knife](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=9)
</row>

</column>

---

This blade or spike can be attached to a crossbow or firearm but, unlike other attached weapons, can be wielded in one hand as its own weapon. When used as a separate weapon, it can't benefit from any runes or abilities that function only for attached weapons. An attached bayonet requires the same number of hands as the weapon it's attached to. A detached bayonet requires one hand.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-9" />
name: Bayonet

<title
    level="1"
    right="Weapon 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Air Repeater](/Weapons.aspx?ID=188)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Agile" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=170" />
<trait label="Repeating" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=374" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 152

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon weapons, ammunition, and related items from Chapter 4_

**Access**  The following regions have access to firearms: Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, Tian Xia, Vudra, Arcadia, Ustalav, the Shackles

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 4 gp

**Damage** 1d4 P

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1

**Range** 30 ft.

**Reload** 0
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Ranged

**Category** Simple

**Group** [Firearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16)
</row>

**Ammunition** [Magazine (Air Repeater)](/Weapons.aspx?ID=209)

</column>

---

A thin-barreled firearm that uses a container of pressurized air instead of black powder to propel small metal bullets from an attached cartridge, the air repeater has fallen out of common use in Arcadia due to its poor stopping power, though it's still used occasionally for casual hunting and sport shooting. The air repeater and its longer-ranged, two-handed variant are still valued by some for their ability to allow a shooter to fire multiple rounds without needing to stop to reload or crank to a new chamber. A typical air repeater magazine holds 6 pellets.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-16" />

<title level="2">Specific Magic Weapons</title>
[Dezullon Fountain](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1883), [Dezullon Fountain](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4416)
name: Air Repeater

[Fire Lance](/Weapons.aspx?ID=190)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Fatal d10" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=178" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 153

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon weapons, ammunition, and related items from Chapter 4_

**Access**  The following regions have access to firearms: Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, Tian Xia, Vudra, Arcadia, Ustalav, the Shackles

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 3 gp

**Damage** 1d6 P

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 2

**Range** 10 ft.

**Reload** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

**Ammunition** [Firearm Ammunition (10 rounds)](/Weapons.aspx?ID=211)

</column>

---

This amazingly simple projectile weapon is nothing more than a metal tube packed with black powder and a stopper, attached to the sharpened head of a javelin. A loaded fire lance can be wielded as a normal [spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10), though it requires an Interact action to regrip the weapon and hold it properly when switching from one use to another. Fire lances are most commonly found in Tian Xia, though occasionally one makes its way all the way to Avistan, typically in the hands of a Tien mercenary or caravan guard.

[Flintlock Musket](/Weapons.aspx?ID=191)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concussive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=401" />
<trait label="Fatal d10" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=178" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 153, [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 50

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon weapons, ammunition, and related items from Chapter 4_

**Favored Weapon** [Cixyron](/Deities.aspx?ID=417)

**Access**  The following regions have access to firearms: Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, Tian Xia, Vudra, Arcadia, Ustalav, the Shackles

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 5 gp

**Damage** 1d6 P

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 2

**Range** 70 ft.

**Reload** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Ranged

**Category** Simple

**Group** [Firearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16)
</row>

**Ammunition** [Firearm Ammunition (10 rounds)](/Weapons.aspx?ID=211)

</column>

---

The most commonly available of firearms in Alkenstar, the flintlock musket includes an external firing mechanism and an efficient and relatively compact frame. Though lacking the range and firing power of the [arquebus](/Weapons.aspx?ID=195) preferred by Alkenstar and Dongun Hold's military members, the flintlock musket is popular among civilians for its ease of use.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Concussive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=401" />
<trait label="Fatal d8" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=178" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 153, [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 50

---

Though less accurate and powerful than a [flintlock musket](/Weapons.aspx?ID=191), the flintlock pistol is a preferred weapon of privateers thanks to its more compact size and affordability. Pirate captains often wear a brace of such pistols in a bandolier so they can draw and fire without stopping to reload.

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

Popular among privateers and mercenaries in Goka, hand cannons are little more than a hardened tube with a handle and external ignition attached. Like the one-handed [air repeater](/Weapons.aspx?ID=188), this thin-barreled firearm uses a container of pressurized air instead of black powder to propel small metal pellets from an attached cartridge. The long air repeater has better range and ammo capacity than the one-handed variant, though it still lacks significant stopping power. A typical long air repeater magazine holds 8 pellets.

The tradition of [dwarves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) displaying their clan affiliations with special [clan daggers](/Weapons.aspx?ID=13) goes back millennia, but many of the dwarf clans of Dongun Hold have their own take on the tradition, with promising young gunsmiths claiming their adulthood by crafting a specialized personal firearm using the clan's unique smithing traditions. Losing or being forced to surrender their clan pistol is a terrible embarrassment for the dwarves that carry them.

This [flintlock pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=192) has two side-by-side barrels. Though less accurate than a standard pistol, a double-barreled pistol is a useful and versatile weapon. It's generally banned in areas where duels with pistols are relatively common, in much the same way that arriving at an aristocratic duel with a [scattergun](/Weapons.aspx?ID=207) would be considered crass, at best.

Similar to the [blunderbuss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=196), a dragon-mouth pistol fires pellets from a flared barrel. Though less powerful than a blunderbuss, the dragon-mouth pistol is appreciated for its portability and one-handed design. Though the name was coined because of the destructive belch of this handheld scatter weapon, many gunsmiths craft dragon-mouth pistols with elaborate embellishments that resemble a stylized dragon's maw framing the barrel.

A favored weapon of monster hunters in Arcadia, the harmona gun is a large-bore long gun that fires a heavy, slow-moving round. The gun got its name due to the eerie similarity between the buzzing sound its oversized projectiles make flying through the air and the flight of a fey bird called a [harmona](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1190).

---

Jezails are simple, efficient long guns developed in Casmaron that typically feature a custom stock and a flintlock firing mechanism. Though lacking the range and stopping power of an [arquebus](/Weapons.aspx?ID=195) or the raw force of a [harmona gun](/Weapons.aspx?ID=202), the jezail is an elegant, well-balanced weapon suitable for a variety of combat situations. It's even possible to tuck it under one arm to fire a less accurate attack that uses only one hand.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

<title
    level="1"
    right="Weapon 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dwarven Scattergun](/Weapons.aspx?ID=207)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concussive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=401" />
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />
<trait label="Kickback" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=409" />
<trait label="Scatter 10 ft." url="/Traits.aspx?ID=413" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 153

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon weapons, ammunition, and related items from Chapter 4_

**Access**  The following regions have access to firearms: Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, Tian Xia, Vudra, Arcadia, Ustalav, the Shackles

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 10 gp

**Damage** 1d8 P

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 2

**Range** 50 ft.

**Reload** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Ranged

**Category** Advanced

**Group** [Firearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16)
</row>

**Ammunition** [Firearm Ammunition (5 rounds)](/Weapons.aspx?ID=212)

</column>

---

A favored weapon of [dwarf](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) scouts from Dongun Hold, the dwarven scattergun is a powerful weapon designed to take advantage of a dwarf's sturdy frame. A dwarven scattergun fires a large paper cartridge stuffed with black powder and knuckle-sized lumps of metal, creating a devastating burst so destructive that a foolish dwarf might find themself catching painful ricochets when firing at a too-close target. Some scatterguns are crafted with a clockwork firing tray that can quickly sort and load black powder and shot without needing a prepackaged cartridge, though this is largely an aesthetic feature with no real mechanical benefit.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-16" />

<title level="2">Specific Magic Weapons</title>
[Animate Dreamer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1177), [Animate Dreamer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4281)

</title>

<traits>

<row gap="medium">

</column>

---

A flingflenser is a [goblin](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=4)-designed weapon ending in an ovoid tube with a hatch and handle on the narrow end. A cluster of circular blades held together and attached to a black powder packet with a thin leather strap serves as ammunition and is loaded through the hatch before being fired with a flintlock or other external ignition mechanism. The flingflenser's sturdy design also places it among the more reliable goblin weapons.

>
[Firearm Ammunition (10 rounds)](/Weapons.aspx?ID=211)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 151

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon weapons, ammunition, and related items from Chapter 4_

**Access**  The following regions have access to firearms: Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, Tian Xia, Vudra, Arcadia, Ustalav, the Shackles

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 1 sp

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Ranged

**Category** Ammunition

**Group** [Firearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16)
</row>

</column>

---

Firearms require ammunition consisting of a projectile and black powder. A round of ammo can vary in its composition but is typically either a prepackaged paper cartridge, including wadding, bullet, and black powder, or loose shot packed in manually. Some weapons, like hand cannons and blunderbusses, can fire other materials, but their ammunition has the same Price due to the cost of the black powder. Because making rounds of firearm ammunition requires creating black powder, you need the [Alchemical Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=752) skill feat to make them. Firearm rounds are a valid option for magical ammunition, just like arrows or bolts. Crafting magical firearm ammunition requires you to be able to craft both alchemical and magical items.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-16" />

    level="1"

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 159

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon weapons, ammunition, and related items from Chapter 4_

**Access**  The following regions have access to firearms: Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, Tian Xia, Vudra, Arcadia, Ustalav, the Shackles

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 8 gp

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Hands** 1

**Category** Martial

</column>

---

This pair of [knuckle dusters](/Weapons.aspx?ID=185) is fitted with an explosive charge of black powder within the hollowed spikes of the weapon and a firing mechanism you hold in your hand while in use.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-16" />
